about.title: About SmartPlay HK OSS | Community Project
about.description: Empowering the Hong Kong sports community with open technology. Learn more about the mission, mechanisms, and the team behind SmartPlay HK OSS.
about.ogDescription: Our mission is to make sports facility information accessible to everyone in Hong Kong.

about.title: About SmartPlay HK OSS
about.subtitle: Empowering Hong Kong's sports community with open technology

mission.title: Our Mission
mission.description: SmartPlay HK OSS exists because the official website can be challenging to navigate and lacks essential features like cross-district monitoring. We believe finding a place to play should be seamless and accessible.

mechanism.title: How it Works
mechanism.description: This tool acts as a specialized client that efficiently queries availability data. It aggregates information in real-time, providing you with a unified, transparent view of sports facilities across Hong Kong.

items.q: Is this an official government website?
items.a: No, this is an independent community-driven open-source project. It is NOT affiliated with, endorsed by, or connected to the Leisure and Cultural Services Department (LCSD) of Hong Kong. We built this project to provide a modern, more accessible interface that overcomes usability limitations of the official system.

items.q: Can I book facilities directly here?
items.a: No. This platform is strictly for checking availability across multiple districts and facilities simultaneously. Due to security protocols and official policy, all actual bookings and payment transactions must be completed on the official LCSD SmartPlay website or mobile app.

Yes, the system fetches data directly from official sources. However, to maintain high performance and minimize unnecessary load on official servers, we use a 30-minute caching strategy for general queries. For time-sensitive booking, we recommend verifying the final status on the official portal.

items.q: Do you store my SmartPlay login credentials?
items.a: Never. SmartPlay HK OSS is built with a 'zero-trust' security model regarding personal data. We do not require, request, or store your SmartPlay account credentials. Our backend is designed only to aggregate and process publicly available facility status information.

items.q: Is this tool free to use?
items.a: Absolutely. This project is 100% free and open-source. It contains no advertisements, no hidden trackers, and no subscription fees. It is maintained by volunteers who believe that information about public facilities should be easily and freely accessible to everyone.

items.q: What is a Webhook and how do I use it?
items.a: A Webhook is a technical bridge that allows our system to send automated, real-time alerts to your own apps (like Discord or Slack). By setting up a Webhook URL, you can receive a notification the very instant a venue becomes available, allowing you to react much faster than manual checking.

items.q: How does the Watcher system work?
items.a: A Watcher is an automated 'scout' that monitors specific venues and time slots for you. Instead of manually refreshing the page, our server checks in the background. If a slot matching your criteria is found, a notification is triggered immediately via your chosen Webhook.

items.q: How often does the system check for availability?
items.a: Most facility availability is scanned every 30 minutes. This interval is carefully chosen to provide reliable updates while ensuring we remain a 'good citizen' of the internet—respecting Hong Kong's official infrastructure by not overwhelming their systems.

items.q: Which platforms are supported for notifications?
items.a: We provide native, pre-formatted support for Discord and Slack to make alerts look clean and readable. For power users, our 'Generic Webhook' option sends standardized JSON data that can be integrated with tools like Zapier, IFTTT, or your own custom bots.

items.q: How can I contribute to the project?
items.a: As an open-source project, we welcome contributions! Whether you are a developer (React/Node.js), a designer, or want to help with translations and documentation, you can contribute by submitting Pull Requests or reporting issues on our GitHub repository found in the footer.
